Politics.headline: Iran fires on targets across the Mideast while Israel and US hit Tehran as war nears end of 5th week
Politics.detail: Iran has fired on targets across the Middle East, damaging a desalination plant and setting a refinery ablaze in Kuwait as the war nears its fifth week.

Politics.headline: Gulf kingdom of Bahrain cracks down on dissent as Iran war reignites internal unrest
Politics.detail: Bahrain is experiencing renewed internal unrest amid the ongoing war with Iran, leading to a crackdown on dissent.

Politics.headline: Trump fires Bondi as attorney general, installs Blanche as acting AG
Politics.detail: President Trump has dismissed Pam Bondi as attorney general and appointed Todd Blanche as acting AG, marking the second cabinet member removal in under a month.

Politics.headline: US judge rules against deporting man whose murder conviction was overturned after 44 years in prison
Politics.detail: A U.S. judge has ruled against the deportation of a man whose murder conviction was overturned after he spent 44 years in prison.

Politics.headline: Trump claims Starmer is weak as he mocks PM and UK aircraft carriers
Politics.detail: Donald Trump has made comments criticizing Keir Starmer, the UK Prime Minister, and UK aircraft carriers.

Finance & Economy.headline: Gov't to prepare more measures to ease consumption strain amid Middle East tensions: Finance chief
Finance & Economy.detail: The South Korean government will introduce additional measures to address potential constraints on consumption stemming from recent energy-saving efforts amid the crisis in the Middle East, according to Finance Minister Koo Yun-cheol, as the Strait of Hormuz has effectively been closed disrupting fuel supplies. The government has rolled out various measures amid the energy crunch, such as a fuel price cap and restrictions on vehicle use in the public sector.

Finance & Economy.headline: China, Canada agree to hold economic, financial strategic dialogue in second half of this year
Finance & Economy.detail: Chinese Vice-Premier He Lifeng and Canadian Finance Minister Francois-Philippe Champagne agreed in Beijing to hold a China-Canada Economic and Financial Strategic Dialogue in the second half of this year. He called for deepened cooperation in economy, trade and finance to foster the stable development of China-Canada economic relations.

Finance & Economy.headline: Central banks' inflation mood puzzle: more judgment than science
Finance & Economy.detail: Central bankers are trying to understand how business executives, labor unions and households are navigating their finances through the energy shock caused by the Iran war. Policymakers are contemplating raising interest rates to combat rising inflation, but will only do so if the energy cost surge filters into other prices, lifting inflation expectations.

Finance & Economy.headline: U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services, February 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: The U.S. monthly international trade deficit increased in February 2026, from $54.7 billion in January to $57.3 billion, as imports increased more than exports. The goods deficit increased $2.5 billion to $84.6 billion, while the services surplus decreased $0.2 billion to $27.3 billion.

Finance & Economy.headline: Canada trade deficit widens as gold drives record imports
Finance & Economy.detail: Canada's trade deficit has widened due to record imports driven by gold, with imports from the U.S. surging by 13.6 per cent to the highest level since March 2025, when tariffs came into effect. The Bank of Canada is also worried about the impact of the oil shock on inflation during rate deliberations, fearing higher gas prices along with already high food inflation will push up consumers' expectations.

Health.headline: Aging Lungs May Play Major Role in Flu and COVID-19 Danger for Older Adults
Health.detail: A new study reveals that aging lungs may play a major role in why flu and COVID can become so dangerous for older adults, as certain lung cells can trigger an exaggerated immune response, creating clusters of inflammatory cells.

Health.headline: New Gene Therapy Gives Deaf People the Chance to Hear
Health.detail: A new gene therapy is giving people born deaf the chance to hear, often within just weeks; researchers delivered a working copy of a key hearing gene directly into the inner ear using a single injection.

Health.headline: New Clinical Trial Suggests Dietary Relief for Crohn's Disease
Health.detail: A new clinical trial suggests that what people eat could finally offer real relief for Crohn's disease, a condition that has long lacked clear dietary guidance; researchers found that a “fasting-mimicking diet” — involving just five days — shows promise.

Health.headline: French Nutrition Expert Targeted by Health Deepfakes
Health.detail: Deepfake videos impersonating Serge Hercberg stayed online for days despite multiple reports, exposing gaps in platform moderation and risks to patient trust.

Health.headline: Aerobics May Up Sleep Quality in Depression, Anxiety
Health.detail: Aerobic exercise may improve sleep quality for individuals experiencing depression and anxiety.

Sports.headline: Records tumble as red-hot Panthers demolish Storm
Sports.detail: The Penrith Panthers have become the first team in NRL history to win their first five games of the season by 20 points or more after their dominant performance against the Melbourne Storm.

Sports.headline: 'Really hard to take': Michael Voss fronts up after Blues' Good Friday 'worst nightmare'
Sports.detail: Carlton coach Michael Voss addressed his team's late-game collapse in their 10-point loss to North Melbourne, acknowledging their inability to finish strongly despite holding a significant lead.

Sports.headline: 'I'm a bit happier, you're lucky': Wayne's classic reaction to Souths win amid touching Merritt tributes
Sports.detail: South Sydney coach Wayne Bennett expressed satisfaction with his team's performance in their victory over the Bulldogs, especially after a less convincing win in their previous game, with the team also honoring former player Nathan Merritt.

Sports.headline: Out of Bounds: Major sports news out of Accra, Los Angeles and Sydney
Sports.detail: This article discusses the establishment of anti-doping regulations in Ghana to promote fair play, Nathan Merritt's attendance at a Rabbitohs game amid his cancer treatment, and more.

Sports.headline: What to know about the Kansas sports authority that will oversee the new Chiefs stadium
Sports.detail: Kansas lawmakers have created an 11-member sports authority to oversee a $3 billion stadium deal aimed at bringing the Kansas City Chiefs to Kansas, addressing financial and legal aspects of the project.

Entertainment.headline: Blake Lively's sexual harassment claims against Justin Baldoni tossed out but robust case remains
Entertainment.detail: A federal judge in New York has tossed out Blake Lively's sexual harassment claims against Justin Baldoni over the movie “It Ends With Us” but left intact claims for retaliation.

Entertainment.headline: Hailee & Josh Welcome 1st Child: Couple Announce Birth of Daughter
Entertainment.detail: Hailee Steinfeld and Josh Allen have announced the birth of their daughter. The couple welcomed their baby girl, announcing the joyful news on Steinfeld's 'Beau Society' blog.

Entertainment.headline: Megan Thee Stallion diagnosed with extreme exhaustion after hospitalization
Entertainment.detail: Megan Thee Stallion was diagnosed with extreme exhaustion after being hospitalized and has taken two Broadway shows off.

Entertainment.headline: Bill Cosby Found Liable in Sexual Assault Case, Owes Victim $59M
Entertainment.detail: Bill Cosby has been found liable in a sexual assault case and is ordered to pay $59 million in damages to Donna Motsinger for drugging and assaulting her in 1972.

Entertainment.headline: Gucci Mane kidnapped, robbed at gunpoint in Dallas, DOJ says
Entertainment.detail: Atlanta rapper Gucci Mane was lured to a Dallas studio for a meeting, then allegedly kidnapped and robbed by a group including rappers Pooh Shiesty and Big30, the DOJ says.

Crime & Justice.headline: 8 arrests made in federal crackdown on alleged health care fraud in Southern California
Crime & Justice.detail: Federal officials have arrested eight people they say were involved in various health care fraud schemes totaling $50 million in and around Los Angeles.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man arrested after threatening an attack on a high-speed train in Germany
Crime & Justice.detail: A man was arrested after threatening an attack on a high-speed train in Germany.

Crime & Justice.headline: FBI arrests 8 for defrauding Medicare
Crime & Justice.detail: The FBI arrested eight people on Thursday for allegedly scheming to defraud Medicare out of more than $50 million.

Crime & Justice.headline: Matt Hancock 'assaulted on London Underground' as man arrested
Crime & Justice.detail: Matt Hancock was reportedly assaulted on the London Underground, and a man has been arrested in connection with the incident.

Crime & Justice.headline: Police slammed as more than 285,000 crimes were not recorded last year
Crime & Justice.detail: Police are facing criticism after reports revealed that more than 285,000 crimes went unrecorded in the past year.

Education.headline: NCERT Enters Higher Education Space With Deemed University Tag
Education.detail: The National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) has been granted the status of a 'Deemed to be University', allowing it to offer its own degrees.

Education.headline: Bengal Board Postpones Interview For Primary Teachers In Election Time
Education.detail: The West Bengal Board of Primary Education has postponed the interview for primary teachers due to the upcoming elections.

Education.headline: Assam Board Result 2026: When Will SEBA Release Class 10, 12 Scorecards?
Education.detail: Students are eagerly waiting for the Assam Board Result 2026 for Class 10 and 12. The Secondary Education Board of Assam (SEBA) is expected to release the scorecards soon.

Education.headline: CBSE Introduces Optional Advanced Levels In Maths And Science From 2026-27
Education.detail: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) will introduce optional advanced levels in Mathematics and Science from the academic year 2026-27.

Education.headline: English Now A Foreign Language In Schools? Here's What CBSE's 3-Language Formula Says
Education.detail: Concerns have arisen regarding the status of English in schools following the implementation of CBSE's 3-language formula.

Lifestyle.headline: JASIWAY's Tartan Plaid Sleeper Chair Emerges as a Breakout Trend in the U.S. Multifunctional Furniture Market for 2026
Lifestyle.detail: Multifunctional furniture is gaining traction in the US due to shrinking living spaces and hybrid lifestyles, with the JASIWAY Sleeper Sofa Chair leading the trend as a versatile 4-in-1 folding guest bed that optimizes limited space without sacrificing style. The global multifunctional furniture market is projected to reach around USD 11.2 billion by 2030, expanding at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of roughly 6.6% between 2024 and 2030.

Lifestyle.headline: Actors Glenn Yong & Eric Lay detail paranormal experiences filming at Batu Caves
Lifestyle.detail: Singaporean actor Glenn Yong shared a spine-chilling experience from his first day of filming at Malaysia's Batu Caves where his camera repeatedly shut off each time he approached a shrine with a prayer altar. Hong Kong actor Philip Keung also faced something unexplainable in Thailand when a potion he was given by a shaman corroded the concrete ground and his shoes.

Lifestyle.headline: Clasp Raised $20 Million To Tackle Healthcare Burnout Through Student Loans
Lifestyle.detail: Clasp, founded by Tess Michaels, pairs soon-to-be medical grads with companies looking to hire and recently announced a $20 million Series B funding round to help solve the 3.2 million-worker shortage in 2026 predicted by the American Hospital Association. The startup aims to help healthcare organizations maintain staffing levels amidst high workforce costs and employee turnover.

Lifestyle.headline: United Airlines checked bag fees climbs $10-50 as fuel prices nearly double since Iran war
Lifestyle.detail: Due to nearly doubled fuel prices since the Iran war, United Airlines is increasing its checked bag fees by $10-$50. This change impacts travelers' experiences and costs associated with air travel.

Lifestyle.headline: Common blood pressure pill could make certain cancer treatments more powerful.
Lifestyle.detail: A common blood pressure pill has the potential to enhance the effectiveness of certain cancer treatments. This finding could significantly impact healthcare and medical treatment approaches.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Trump's Iran blindspot
Opinion/Editorial.detail: President Trump's address to the nation on the war with Iran had qualities of his social media rants but no clarity, failing to articulate clear goals and a rallying point for Americans amidst rising oil prices and stock market declines. The editorial criticizes Trump for suggesting the U.S. is self-sufficient in oil and calling on European allies to step up military commitments in the Strait of Hormuz.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion | If Congress wants dialogue with Russia, it's talking to the wrong people
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The opinion piece suggests that instead of engaging with Putin-friendly lawmakers, Congress should create a Free Russia Caucus to foster dialogue with Russia's pro-democracy movement and civil society. This would reaffirm American principles and contribute to democratic reform in a post-Putin Russia.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: OPINION - Hyper-connectivity put to test: Israel/US and Iran war and Türkiye's critical role
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The article discusses the risk to fiber-optic cables in the Bab al-Mandeb Strait due to the war, emphasizing that the world may be ignoring a potentially devastating data crisis. It suggests that Türkiye, with its fiber-optic networks, can play a critical role in safeguarding regional stability and strengthening its digital sovereignty.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: ED Tightening pressure on Korea
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Amidst the war in Iran and a declining domestic economy, President Trump is increasing pressure on South Korea regarding energy, security and trade. Trump expressed frustration that nations including Korea were not dispatching ships and personnel to manage the Strait of Hormuz.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: China tightening its grip on the KMT
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial discusses China's efforts to influence Taiwan through the Chinese Nationalist Party (KMT), with President Xi Jinping inviting KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun to visit China. Cheng's acceptance and adherence to the "1992 consensus" are seen as a risk to Taiwan's sovereignty and could potentially backfire on the KMT in local elections.

World.headline: Iran fires on targets across the Mideast while Israel and US hit Tehran as war nears end of 5th week
World.detail: Iran has fired on targets across the Middle East, damaging a desalination plant and setting a refinery ablaze in Kuwait.

World.headline: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing appointed president after 'sham' election
World.detail: Min Aung Hlaing seized control five years ago and plunged Myanmar into conflict and economic chaos.

World.headline: Water plant hit in Kuwait as Iran attacks Gulf, Israel after Trump threats
World.detail: Kuwait says desalination plant damaged after earlier assault on oil refinery.

World.headline: Artemis II on course for the moon after day in Earth orbit
World.detail: The move to blast out of Earth's orbit puts the astronauts on a trajectory that humans haven't traveled in more than a half-century.

World.headline: Gulf states face wave of attacks as Iran warns of strikes on US-linked assets
World.detail: Iran condemns what it called an Israeli attack on Kuwait's desalination plant.

Zimbabwe.headline: Chamisa fumes over Mnangagwa term chaos
Zimbabwe.detail: Nelson Chamisa has expressed outrage over the proposed extension of President Mnangagwa's term, calling it a move designed to keep him in power without facing elections. The controversial Constitutional Amendment Bill has faced opposition from various groups.

Zimbabwe.headline: Bulawayo water rationing remains in force
Zimbabwe.detail: Water rationing continues to be implemented in Bulawayo. Residents are still facing challenges related to water access.

Zimbabwe.headline: NetOne, ZTA ink landmark partnership to modernise Zim's tourism sector
Zimbabwe.detail: NetOne and the Zimbabwe Tourism Authority (ZTA) have partnered to modernize Zimbabwe's tourism sector. This collaboration aims to leverage technology to enhance the tourism experience.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mthuli eyes bridge financing to pay Zim's debt
Zimbabwe.detail: Finance Minister Mthuli Ncube is considering bridge financing options to manage Zimbabwe's debt. The government is exploring ways to address its financial obligations.

Zimbabwe.headline: CBZ secures US$1:ZiG1 debt shield amid expansion drive
Zimbabwe.detail: CBZ has secured a US$1:ZiG1 debt shield as part of its expansion efforts. This move is intended to protect the company's assets amidst currency fluctuations.
